exam.title: MSc Admission Test Preparation
instructions: Answer all questions of the following fifty questions.
The figures shown in the right margin indicate full marks. There are no negative markings.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What will be the value of z?
questions.explanation: (x=18) assigns 18, a nonzero (true) value, so the conditional takes the first branch and evaluates y=48, making z = 48.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Find output:
questions.explanation: pptr holds the address of ptr (it is a pointer-to-pointer), so printing pptr shows the address of ptr.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is the output of the following C code?
questions.explanation: With integer arithmetic and implicit multiplication, 2/3 = 0 so (6+0)=6, 5*6 = 30, then 3*2/30 = 6/30 = 0, leaving 9 + 0 = 9.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: How many times recursive function calls for the following code?
questions.explanation: With T(n)=1+T(n-1)+T(n-3) and base case 1 for n≤0: T(1)=3, T(2)=5, T(3)=7, T(4)=11, T(5)=17 total calls.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which of the following OOP mechanisms explicitly ensures data security by preventing direct outside access to data members, allowing manipulation only through a well-defined public interface?
questions.explanation: Encapsulation hides internal data behind a public interface, restricting direct external access.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Consider the following declaration block in a standard C++ application. If no access modifier is explicitly mentioned, what is the default accessibility profile of the member variable roll and the function setRoll()?

options.c: roll is private and setRoll() is public

questions.explanation: Members of a C++ class default to private access when no specifier is given, so both roll and setRoll() are private.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which component of the Java Development Kit (JDK) is explicitly responsible for translating human-readable .java source files into platform-independent .class bytecode files?
questions.explanation: javac compiles .java source files into .class bytecode; the JVM later executes that bytecode.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Consider the following Java code snippet. What will be the exact console output?
questions.explanation: s1 == s2 compares references (different objects → false); equals() compares contents (same text → true), so 'false true'.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which of the following conditions must be met for a Java interface to be targeted and safely instantiated using a standard Lambda expression?

options.a: It must contain only private helper methods.
options.b: It must contain exactly one abstract method.
options.c: It must extend the Runnable interface.
options.d: It must be marked explicitly with the final keyword modifier.

questions.explanation: A lambda targets a functional interface, which by definition has exactly one abstract method.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A graph has 24 edges and degree of each vertex is k, then which of the following is possible number of vertices?
questions.explanation: By the handshaking lemma, k·V = 2·24 = 48. Only V = 8 (with k = 6) divides 48 to an integer degree among the options.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the minimum number of people required in a room to guarantee that at least two of them share the exact same birth month?
questions.explanation: By the pigeonhole principle, with 12 months you need 12 + 1 = 13 people to force a shared month.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Consider the compound proposition (p ∧ q) → p. Which of the following best describes this logical statement?

options.d: It is valid only when p is false.

questions.explanation: Whenever (p ∧ q) is true, p is true, so the implication is true in every case — a tautology.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which data structure is primarily used to implement Breadth-First Search (BFS) traversal of a graph?
questions.explanation: BFS visits nodes level by level using a FIFO queue to track the frontier.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: If the inorder traversal of a binary tree is B, A, C and its preorder traversal is A, B, C, what is the postorder traversal of the tree?
questions.explanation: Preorder makes A the root; inorder splits B (left) and C (right). Postorder (left, right, root) is B, C, A.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which data structure is ideal for checking balanced parentheses in an expression (e.g., matching opening and closing brackets)?
questions.explanation: A stack matches each closing bracket with the most recent unmatched opening bracket (LIFO).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: What is the tightest upper bound (O) of the following loop block?
questions.explanation: The outer loop runs O(log n) times (i doubles) and the inner loop runs O(n) times, giving O(n log n).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Using the Master Method, what is the asymptotic time complexity of the recurrence relation T(n) = 2T(n/2) + Theta(n)?
questions.explanation: This is Master Theorem case 2 (work matches n^{log_b a} = n), giving Θ(n log n) — the merge-sort recurrence.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of the Quick Sort algorithm when the pivot chosen is consistently the smallest or largest element?
questions.explanation: A consistently extreme pivot produces maximally unbalanced partitions, degrading quicksort to O(n²).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: The Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) problem is efficiently solved using which of the following approaches?
questions.explanation: LCS has overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure, so it is solved with dynamic programming in O(mn).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which of the following single-source shortest path algorithms can correctly handle graphs containing negative weight edges, provided there are no negative weight cycles?
questions.explanation: Bellman-Ford handles negative edge weights (and detects negative cycles); Dijkstra requires non-negative weights.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Which of the following issues in a traditional file processing system is directly caused by the lack of central control over data storage and formatting?

options.b: Poor execution speed of sequential searches
options.d: Inability to store text files alongside numeric data

questions.explanation: Without central control, the same data is duplicated across files and can diverge, causing redundancy and inconsistency.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: The Relational Algebra operator that outputs all tuples from relation R that do not have a matching match in relation S across shared attributes is known as:
questions.explanation: Tuples of R with no match in S are obtained via set difference (the basis of the anti-join).

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: If a relation is in 2nd Normal Form (2NF), which of the following vulnerabilities has been completely eliminated?

options.b: Partial functional dependencies on a candidate key

questions.explanation: 2NF removes partial dependencies of non-prime attributes on part of a candidate key; transitive dependencies go in 3NF.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: In a standard B+ Tree index structure used by database storage engines, actual data record pointers or actual data rows are exclusively stored in which locations?

options.d: Evenly distributed across all levels of the tree

questions.explanation: In a B+ tree, internal nodes only route searches; all data pointers/records live in the leaf nodes.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: An attacker intercepts network traffic to analyze packet headers and discover communication patterns between two servers without altering the data. This is an example of which type of attack?
questions.explanation: Observing/analyzing traffic without modifying it is a passive attack (eavesdropping / traffic analysis).

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: What is the primary objective of a Domain Name System (DNS) poisoning attack?

options.a: To intercept session cookies from a user's browser
options.b: To corrupt database tables by injecting malicious SQL code
options.c: To redirect users to a malicious website by modifying IP mapping records
options.d: To flood a server with synchronization requests to crash it

questions.explanation: DNS poisoning corrupts name-to-IP mappings so victims are redirected to attacker-controlled sites.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which type of malware appears to be a legitimate, useful software application but performs malicious actions in the background when executed by the user?
questions.explanation: A Trojan horse masquerades as benign software while carrying out hidden malicious behaviour.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: A communication channel is completely noiseless and has a bandwidth of 4 kHz. If the digital signal uses 4 discrete voltage levels to represent data, what is the maximum bit rate achievable according to the Nyquist formula?
questions.explanation: Nyquist: rate = 2·B·log₂(L) = 2·4000·log₂(4) = 2·4000·2 = 16 kbps.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which physical topology requires a central controller or hub to which all dedicated links from devices are connected, offering easy installation but presenting a single point of failure?
questions.explanation: In a star topology every device links to a central hub, which is also a single point of failure.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which line coding scheme has no DC component and provides synchronization?
questions.explanation: Manchester encoding has a mid-bit transition for every bit, eliminating DC and providing self-synchronization.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: What is the primary function of ARP?

options.a: Find MAC address from IP address
options.b: Find IP address from MAC address

questions.explanation: The Address Resolution Protocol maps a known IP address to its corresponding MAC (hardware) address.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which protocol allows users to keep emails on the mail server and synchronize multiple devices?
questions.explanation: IMAP keeps mail on the server and synchronizes state across devices; POP3 typically downloads and removes it.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which routing algorithm requires each router to have complete knowledge of the network topology?
questions.explanation: Link-state routing floods link information so every router builds a full map of the topology and runs Dijkstra.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: In the instruction cycle, which step comes immediately after fetching an instruction?
questions.explanation: After fetch, the control unit decodes the instruction to determine the operation and operands.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: A processor has a clock rate of 2 GHz and an average CPI of 2. What is the execution time for 1000 instructions?
questions.explanation: Time = (instructions × CPI) / clock = (1000 × 2) / (2×10⁹) = 1×10⁻⁶ s = 1 µs.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: In the classic 5-stage MIPS pipeline, which stage performs arithmetic and logical operations?
questions.explanation: The Execute (EX) stage contains the ALU that performs arithmetic and logical operations.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: Which instruction format generally requires the fewest bits to specify operands?
questions.explanation: Zero-address (stack) instructions take operands implicitly from the stack, needing no operand fields.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which design pattern ensures that only one instance of a class exists throughout the system?
questions.explanation: The Singleton pattern restricts a class to a single shared instance with a global access point.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which software development model is primarily risk-driven?
questions.explanation: The Spiral model centres each iteration on risk analysis, making it the risk-driven process model.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which development approach welcomes changing requirements even at a late stage of development?
questions.explanation: Agile explicitly welcomes changing requirements, even late in development, to give the customer advantage.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: In the FCFS scheduling algorithm, the main disadvantage is:

options.c: Convoy effect due to long processes

questions.explanation: Under FCFS a long job at the front makes short jobs wait — the convoy effect — hurting average waiting time.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Three processes arrive at time 0 with burst times: P1 = 8 ms, P2 = 4 ms, P3 = 2 ms. According to the Shortest Job First (SJF) scheduling algorithm, which process executes first?
questions.explanation: SJF runs the shortest burst first; P3 (2 ms) is the shortest, so it executes first.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Which page replacement algorithm can never produce more page faults when the number of page frames is increased?
questions.explanation: LRU is a stack algorithm, so it never suffers Belady's anomaly — more frames never increase faults.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: Which of the following grammars is NOT LL(1)?
questions.explanation: S → aA | aB has two productions starting with 'a' (overlapping FIRST sets), so it is not LL(1).

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: Which of the following statements is TRUE regarding DFA and NFA?

options.a: Every NFA can be converted to an equivalent DFA.
options.b: Every DFA can recognize more languages than an NFA.
options.c: NFA requires less memory during execution than DFA in all cases.
options.d: DFA may have multiple transitions for the same input symbol from a state.

questions.explanation: Via the subset construction, every NFA has an equivalent DFA; the two models recognize the same languages.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: Which of the following is the simplified form of F(A, B) = Σm(1, 2, 3)?
questions.explanation: Σm(1,2,3) = A'B + AB' + AB = A + B (only minterm 0, A'B', is excluded).

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: The carry-out C_out of a full adder is equal to:
questions.explanation: A full adder's carry-out is the majority function AB + B·C_in + A·C_in; option (a) is the sum bit.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: In the Minimax algorithm with Alpha-Beta pruning, pruning occurs when:

options.b: Beta becomes less than or equal to Alpha

questions.explanation: When β ≤ α the remaining branches cannot affect the outcome, so they are pruned (the α-β cutoff).

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Which of the following best describes overfitting in a machine learning model?

options.a: Model performs well on both training and unseen data
options.b: Model is too simple to capture data patterns
options.c: Model performs well on training data but poorly on unseen data

questions.explanation: An overfit model memorizes training noise, so it scores high on training data but generalizes poorly.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: Two fair dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum is 7?
questions.explanation: Six of the 36 equally likely outcomes sum to 7, so the probability is 6/36 = 1/6.
